name: Stir Fried Rice Noodles with Tofu & Egg (Pad Thai)
recipeIngredient: 1/2 Pound Thai Flat Rice Noodles (bahn pho)
1/2 Pound Firm Tofu, cut into small cubes
1/2 Cup Skinless Boneless Chicken Breast, very thinly sliced
1/4 Pound Shrimp, peeled and cut into half-length pieces
3 Tablespoons Dry-Roasted Salted Peanuts, finely chopped
2 Teaspoons Red Chili Paste with Garlic
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the Tamarind sauce (see notes) and set it aside to cool.
2. Soak the noodles in warm water until pliable, about 15 minutes. Drain and set aside. Place the noodles in boiling water and cook just until the water returns to a boil. Drain again. Heat oil for deep-frying to 375°F (190°C) and deep-fry tofu. Pat tofu dry on paper towels first to prevent spattering. Drain tofu and set aside.
3. Heat peanut oil in a large wok, add garlic, and stir-fry for a few minutes. Add chicken, shrimp, drained noodles, beaten eggs, and deep-fried tofu. Toss well and stir-fry over medium-high heat for 3-4 minutes until the noodles are hot and tender.
4. Add remaining ingredients except garnishes and stir-fry for a few more minutes.
5. Serve on a platter with garnishes, which are an essential part of the dish. Serves 4-6.
Available at some Asian markets
Notes: Unable to find shrimp powder; use dried shrimp ground in a food processor.
